title: navarretia nigelliformis subsp. radians
intro: Navarretia nigelliformis subsp. radians, also known as the coastal or radiating pincushion plant, is a flowering plant that belongs to the phlox family (Polemoniaceae) and is native to the coastal regions of California. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and skin infections. The health benefits of Navarretia nigelliformis subsp. radians include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
